entries.title: 1.8L Petrol
entries.history: Primary 1.8L engine for Wide Body Town variants; known for head gasket issues addressed by Mitsubishi service bulletin MB998539.

entries.history: Larger displacement option for Town; timing belt driven requiring scheduled replacement to prevent interference damage.

Hydraulic lash adjuster wear causing valve noise

entries.title: 1.3L Petrol
entries.history: Entry-level engine for standard BJ Town; timing chain design but prone to tensioner wear on high-mileage examples.

Valve guide wear on high-mileage units
EGR valve clogging on later models
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4G13 Mitsubishi Town engine replacement

Turbo oil seal leaks on high-mileage units

commonProblems.h2: Mitsubishi Town Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mitsubishi Town H80 (1998-2006) relies on proven but ageing Mitsubishi engine families. The 4A30 petrol and 4D68 diesel dominate UK replacement enquiries due to head gasket and EGR cooler failures respectively. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Town 4A30 Head Gasket Failure - Why Mitsubishi Town Engines Overheat
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi Town BJ, Wide Body, Soft Top (1998-2006, 4A30 1.8L Petrol)
problems.rootCause: Original head gasket (MB998539) deteriorates under heat cycles; cooling system weakness accelerates failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, replace gasket MB998539, resurface head, refill coolant
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if block is undamaged; 20k-40k mile expectation

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded gasket, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Restores like-new reliability; recommended for Town worth £2k+

problems.recommendation: On a Mitsubishi Town valued at £1,500-£3,000, a full rebuilt engine at £1,800-£2,200 often represents better long-term value than a head gasket repair that may not address underlying cooling weaknesses. We recommend rebuilt units with upgraded components for JDM import owners.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 4A30 engine prices for your Mitsubishi Town

problems.h4: Town 4D68 EGR Cooler Failure - Diesel Coolant Loss Explained
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi Town Van, Wide Body Van (2000-2006, 4D68 2.5L Diesel)
problems.rootCause: EGR cooler (part MR202287) develops cracks from thermal cycling, allowing exhaust gases into coolant system.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace EGR cooler MR202287, flush cooling system, test for leaks
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses symptom only; cooler may fail again in 20k miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt diesel engine with revised EGR components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Comprehensive solution for commercial van users needing reliability

problems.recommendation: For Town vans used in urban delivery work, the rebuilt engine option at £2,000-£2,400 provides peace of mind versus repeated EGR repairs. We recommend rebuilt units with upgraded cooling components for high-mileage commercial use.
problems.cta: Get quotes for 4D68 Mitsubishi Town diesel engine replacement

problems.h4: Town 4G13 Timing Chain Wear - Cold Start Rattle Diagnosis
problems.rootCause: Timing chain tensioner loses pressure over time; chain slap damages guides and can jump teeth.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace chain, tensioner, guides; reset timing; test run
repairOptions.longevity: Preventative fix viable if caught before valve damage; 40k+ mile expectation

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded timing components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if chain has jumped or valve damage suspected

problems.recommendation: If your Town 1.3L shows cold-start rattle but runs normally, a timing chain kit at £600-£950 is cost-effective prevention. If misfires or loss of power accompany the rattle, replacement is the safer investment.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 4G13 engine prices for your Mitsubishi Town

problems.h4: Town 4G63 Turbo Failure - Performance Variant Boost Loss
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi Town CZT Turbo (2003-2006, 4G63 2.0L Turbo Petrol)
problems.rootCause: Turbocharger bearings wear from heat soak; intercooler piping cracks under boost pressure cycles.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbo, rebuild or replace cartridge, inspect intercooler piping, test boost
repairOptions.longevity: Viable for enthusiasts maintaining original engine; requires specialist

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt turbo engine with upgraded turbo, intercooler, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for daily-driven performance Towns; ensures reliability

problems.recommendation: Given the specialist nature of the 4G63 turbo system and the Town CZT's enthusiast value, a rebuilt turbo engine at £2,300-£2,700 often represents better value than repeated turbo repairs. We recommend sourcing from JDM turbo specialists.
problems.cta: Get quotes for 4G63 Mitsubishi Town turbo engine replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mitsubishi Town
finalCta.paragraph: The Mitsubishi Town's compact H80 chassis and JDM import status mean engine failures can feel catastrophic for owners. EngineMarket connects you with UK specialists who understand Town variants, engine codes, and the upgraded components needed to prevent repeat failures. Compare rebuilt, reconditioned and used engine prices from vetted suppliers, all backed by a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mitsubishi Town Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mitsubishi Town and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

items.title: Mitsubishi Town 1.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.3L 4G13 was the entry-level petrol engine for standard BJ Town variants, offering adequate power for urban use with simple maintenance requirements.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain tensioner wear causing cold-start rattle; valve guide wear on high-mileage units

items.title: Mitsubishi Town 1.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.5L 4G15 with VVT provided a mid-range option for Soft Top variants, balancing fuel economy with improved low-end torque for open-top driving.
commonFailurePoints: Hydraulic lash adjuster failure causing valve train noise; VVT solenoid clogging from neglected oil changes

items.title: Mitsubishi Town 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.8L 4A30 was the most common petrol engine across Wide Body and Soft Top Town variants, though head gasket failure (MB998539) is a well-documented issue.
commonFailurePoints: Head gasket failure at 60k-90k miles; cooling system weakness leading to overheating

items.title: Mitsubishi Town 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0L 4A31 offered increased displacement for BJ and Wide Body variants, using a timing belt that requires scheduled replacement to prevent interference damage.
commonFailurePoints: Timing belt failure if not replaced at 60k miles; hydraulic lash adjuster wear

keyChanges: H80 chassis introduced for kei-class Town range
4G13 1.3L petrol fitted to standard BJ variants
4A30 1.8L petrol fitted to Wide Body variants
knownFor: Simple, robust engines with basic electronics
Early examples show timing chain tensioner wear on 4G13
Head gasket issues emerge on 4A30 after 60k miles

Soft Top body style launched with 4G15 1.5L and 4A30 1.8L options
4A31 2.0L petrol added for higher-spec BJ variants
knownFor: 4D68 EGR cooler cracking becomes documented failure pattern
Soft Top variants popular with JDM import enthusiasts
4A31 timing belt requires strict 60k mile replacement

years.ticker: Town 4D68 EGR cooler failure • Town Soft Top engine replacement • Town van diesel engine cost

Final H80 production years saw refined cooling systems
Last UK JDM imports registered in 2006

faq.h2: Mitsubishi Town Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: Is the Mitsubishi Town 4A30 engine reliable?
items.answer: The 4A30 1.8L petrol is generally robust but prone to head gasket failure (part MB998539) between 60k-90k miles, a known issue addressed by Mitsubishi service bulletins.
keyPoints: Head gasket failure is the most common 4A30 issue
Upgraded gaskets in rebuilt engines prevent repeat failures
Cooling system maintenance extends engine life significantly
Rebuilt 4A30 units cost £1,800-£2,200 supply only
items.cta: Compare rebuilt 4A30 engine prices for your Mitsubishi Town

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Mitsubishi Town engine in the UK?
items.answer: Rebuilt Mitsubishi Town engines typically cost £1,600-£2,700 supply only, depending on engine code, with 4G13 at the lower end and 4G63 turbo at the premium end.
keyPoints: 4G13 1.3L petrol: £1,600-£1,900 rebuilt
4A30 1.8L petrol: £1,800-£2,200 rebuilt
4D68 2.5L diesel: £2,000-£2,400 rebuilt
4G63 2.0L turbo: £2,300-£2,700 rebuilt
items.cta: Get tailored Mitsubishi Town engine replacement quotes today

items.question: Where can I buy a Mitsubishi Town engine in the UK?
items.answer: UK availability is limited to specialist JDM import suppliers and engine rebuilders who understand H80 chassis compatibility across BJ, Wide Body and Soft Top variants.
keyPoints: Standard breakers rarely stock Town-specific engines
JDM import specialists hold 4A30, 4G13 and 4D68 stock
Rebuilt engines with upgraded components are preferred over used
EngineMarket connects you with vetted UK Town specialists
items.cta: Find Mitsubishi Town engine suppliers through our comparison tool

items.question: Will a non-Town Mitsubishi engine fit my H80 chassis Town?
items.answer: Cross-compatibility is limited; engines must match H80 chassis mounting points, ECU wiring and variant-specific ancillaries for BJ, Wide Body or Soft Top models.
keyPoints: 4A30 engines swap between BJ/Wide Body/Soft Top with minor mods
Turbo 4G63 requires CZT-specific wiring and intercooler
Diesel 4D68 needs van-specific mounts and exhaust routing
Always verify compatibility by registration before ordering
items.cta: Verify engine compatibility for your Mitsubishi Town registration

items.question: How long does a Mitsubishi Town engine last with proper maintenance?
items.answer: Well-maintained Town engines can reach 100k-120k miles, but known failure patterns like 4A30 head gaskets often require intervention at 60k-90k miles.
keyPoints: Regular oil changes prevent 4G13 timing chain wear
Cooling system flushes extend 4A30 head gasket life
EGR cooler replacement prevents 4D68 diesel overheating
Rebuilt engines with upgraded components restore like-new longevity
items.cta: Compare long-life rebuilt Mitsubishi Town engine options

items.question: When should I replace the head gasket on a Town 4A30 engine?
items.answer: Replace the 4A30 head gasket (MB998539) preventatively at 60k miles or immediately if you observe coolant loss, white smoke or overheating symptoms.
keyPoints: Mitsubishi service bulletin MB998539 addresses gasket design
White exhaust smoke indicates coolant entering combustion chamber
Overheating accelerates gasket failure; address cooling issues first
Rebuilt engines include upgraded gaskets as standard
items.cta: Get quotes for 4A30 head gasket upgrade or full replacement

items.question: Can I upgrade my Mitsubishi Town engine for more performance?
items.answer: Enthusiasts can fit the 4G63 turbo engine to non-CZT Towns, but this requires specialist fabrication, ECU remapping and cooling system upgrades.
keyPoints: 4G63 turbo swap is complex and costly (£3k+ for parts and labour)
Cooling system must be upgraded to handle turbo heat
ECU and wiring harness require CZT-specific modifications
Rebuilt CZT engines with warranty are safer than DIY swaps
items.cta: Enquire about Mitsubishi Town performance engine upgrades

items.question: Where is the engine code stamped on a Mitsubishi Town H80?
items.answer: The engine code is stamped on the block near the cylinder head mating surface, visible after removing the air intake on most H80 Town variants.
keyPoints: 4A30 codes appear on the front passenger side of the block
4D68 diesel codes are on the driver side near the injector pump
Clean the area with degreaser to read stamped characters clearly
Photograph the code when ordering replacement parts or engines
items.cta: Identify your Mitsubishi Town engine code with our registration tool

items.question: What warranty do reconditioned Mitsubishi Town engines include?
items.answer: Reconditioned Town engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialist rebuilders.
keyPoints: Warranty covers manufacturing defects and component failure
Labour for warranty claims may require approved fitting centres
Keep service records to maintain warranty validity
Confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier before purchase
items.cta: Compare warranty terms for Mitsubishi Town engine replacements

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a Mitsubishi Town?
items.answer: For Towns valued at £1,500-£4,000, a rebuilt engine at £1,600-£2,700 often represents better value than repeated repairs or purchasing another JDM import.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines restore reliability for daily-driven Towns
Upgraded components address known failure patterns
12-month warranty provides peace of mind versus used engines
Supply & fit options simplify the replacement process
